input_row.title: Ensure backup data in Recovery Services vaults is encrypted using customer-managed keys (CMK)
input_row.description: Recovery Services vaults offer two encryption options: Microsoft-managed keys, which provide automatic encryption without user intervention, and customer-managed keys (CMK), which allow organizations to retain full control over their encryption keys for enhanced security and compliance.
input_row.rationale: Using customer-managed keys (CMKs) to encrypt Recovery Services vaults enhances security by granting organizations complete control over their encryption keys. Impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a Recovery Services vault. 3. Under Settings, click Properties. 4. Under Encryption Settings, click Update. 5. Ensure the box next to Use your own key is checked, and a key URI is displayed under Encryption key. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each Recovery Services vault. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: az backup vault list For each Recovery Services vault, run the following command: az backup vault encryption show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault> Ensure that under properties, encryptionAtRestType is set to CustomerManaged, and a key keyUri exists with the value set to a customer-managed key URI.  Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault Run the following command to get the Recovery Services vault in a resource group with a given name: $vault = Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> - Name <recovery-services-vault> Run the following command to get the encryption setting for the Recovery Services vault: $vault.Properties.EncryptionProperty.KeyVaultProperties Ensure that the command returns a customer-managed key URI. Repeat for each Recovery Services vault. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 2e94d99a-8a36-4563-bc77-810d8893b671 • Name: '[Preview]: Azure Recovery Services vaults should use customer- managed keys for encrypting backup data'
input_row.remediation: Note: Once encryption is configured to use a customer-managed key, this setting cannot be reversed. Remediate from Azure Portall 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a Recovery Services vault. 3. Under Settings, click Properties. 4. Under Encryption Settings, click Update. 5. Check the box next to Use your own key. 6. Under Encryption key, click the radio button next to Enter key URI to provide a known key URI, or click the radio button next to Select from Key Vault to select a key from a Key Vault. 7. If entering a key URI, provide the key URI in the text box under Key URI. 8. If selecting a key from a Key Vault, click select key from Key Vault. 1. Select Key vault or Managed HSM. 2. Select a key vault or managed HSM. 3. Select a key. 4. Click Select. 9. Select a managed identity to use for the encryption key. 10. Click Update. 11. Repeat steps 1-10 for each Recovery Services vault. Remediate from Azure CLI For each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a customer-managed encryption key: az backup vault encryption update --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault> --encryption-key-id <cmk-uri> --mi-system-assigned Note: Use --mi-user-assigned with the above command to provide a UserAssigned Identity Id. Remediate from PowerShell For each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a customer-managed encryption key: Set-AzRecoveryServicesVaultProperty -VaultId <recovery-services-vault-id> - EncryptionKeyId <cmk-uri> -UseSystemAssignedIdentity $true Note: Use -UseSystemAssignedIdentity $false with the above command and - UserAssignedIdentity to provide a UserAssigned Identity Id. Default Value: By default, data in the Recovery Services vault is encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/backup/vault 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/get- azrecoveryservicesvault 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/set- azrecoveryservicesvaultproperty 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure- resources/how-manage-user-assigned-managed-identities Additional Information: • This feature can only be applied to new Recovery Services vaults. Unfortunately, vaults that currently contain existing items that are registered or have previously attempted registration are not supported. • To enable encryption, it is necessary to grant the Recovery Services vault the appropriate permissions to access the encryption key in the key vault. The key can be modified as needed. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with- cmk#assign-permissions-to-the-recovery-services-vault-to-access-the- encryption-key-in-azure-key-vault. • Azure Backup uses system-assigned managed identities and user-assigned managed identities to authenticate the Recovery Services vault to access encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with- cmk#enable-a-managed-identity-for-your-recovery-services-vault.
input_row.impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/backup/vault 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/get- azrecoveryservicesvault 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/set- azrecoveryservicesvaultproperty 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure- resources/how-manage-user-assigned-managed-identities Additional Information: • This feature can only be applied to new Recovery Services vaults. Unfortunately, vaults that currently contain existing items that are registered or have previously attempted registration are not supported. • To enable encryption, it is necessary to grant the Recovery Services vault the appropriate permissions to access the encryption key in the key vault. The key can be modified as needed. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with- cmk#assign-permissions-to-the-recovery-services-vault-to-access-the- encryption-key-in-azure-key-vault. • Azure Backup uses system-assigned managed identities and user-assigned managed identities to authenticate the Recovery Services vault to access encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with- cmk#enable-a-managed-identity-for-your-recovery-services-vault.

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The control can be fully verified using Azure APIs and CLI commands as outlined in the audit section.
- The encryption settings and customer-managed keys for Recovery Services vaults are standard properties that can be queried via Azure APIs or Azure CLI.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_backup_recovery_services_vault_cmk_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

1. **Azure Resource Manager APIs/CLI Commands**:
   - Use the Azure CLI command `az backup vault list` to enumerate all Recovery Services vaults in the specific subscription.
   - For each Recovery Services vault, run the command `az backup vault encryption show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault>` to obtain the encryption settings.

2. **Resources to Enumerate**:
   - Query all Recovery Services vaults within the specified subscription and associated resource groups.

3. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
   - Check the `encryptionAtRestType` property to confirm it is set to `CustomerManaged`.
   - Validate that there is a `keyUri` present, which indicates that a customer-managed key is being used.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values**:
   - **Pass**: 
     - `encryptionAtRestType` is `CustomerManaged`
     - `keyUri` is present and not null
   - **Fail**:
     - `encryptionAtRestType` is not `CustomerManaged`
     - `keyUri` is missing or null

5. **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command**:
   - Azure CLI command for listing vaults: `az backup vault list`
   - Azure CLI command for checking encryption: `az backup vault encryption show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault>` 

This automated approach allows for efficient compliance checks across numerous Recovery Services vaults without manual intervention.

second_opinion.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be effectively verified using Azure APIs and CLI commands, which aligns with the automated approach. Azure provides comprehensive API and CLI support for querying encryption settings of Recovery Services vaults.
- **Azure APIs/CLI Capability:** The use of Azure CLI commands such as `az backup vault list` and `az backup vault encryption show` is appropriate for this task. These commands are capable of retrieving the necessary information to verify the encryption settings.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are technically accurate. The commands listed are correct for enumerating Recovery Services vaults and checking their encryption settings. The criteria for pass/fail based on `encryptionAtRestType` and `keyUri` are also correctly defined.
- **Program Name Convention:** The program name `azure_backup_recovery_services_vault_cmk_enabled` follows a logical naming convention that reflects the service, resource, and security intent.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Additional Considerations:** It would be beneficial to include error handling and logging in the automation script to capture any exceptions or failures during execution. This would enhance the robustness of the automated checks.
- **Alternative Approach:** Consider integrating PowerShell cmdlets for environments where PowerShell is preferred over CLI. The `Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault` and `Get-AzRecoveryServicesVaultEncryptionConfig` cmdlets can be used for similar checks.
- **Documentation and Reporting:** Ensure that the automation script includes detailed documentation and generates a report summarizing the compliance status of each Recovery Services vault.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- I have high confidence in this assessment due to the accurate use of Azure CLI commands and the clear definition of pass/fail criteria. The approach is well-aligned with Azure's capabilities for managing and auditing encryption settings.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is

- The current assessment is technically sound and appropriately classified as automated. The use of Azure CLI for verifying encryption settings is efficient and aligns with best practices for automated compliance checks. No reclassification or significant modifications are necessary.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be effectively verified using Azure APIs and CLI commands. The necessary encryption settings for Recovery Services vaults are accessible via Azure CLI, specifically through the `az backup vault list` and `az backup vault encryption show` commands. These commands provide the required information to determine if customer-managed keys (CMKs) are being used, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_backup_recovery_services_vault_cmk_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Recovery Services Vaults
- **API/CLI Method:** `az backup vault list`, `az backup vault encryption show`
- **Properties to Check:** `encryptionAtRestType`, `keyUri`
- **Pass Criteria:** `encryptionAtRestType` is set to `CustomerManaged`, and a valid `keyUri` is present.
- **Fail Criteria:** `encryptionAtRestType` is not `CustomerManaged`, or `keyUri` is missing or invalid.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported the automated approach based on the capabilities of Azure CLI and APIs.
